channel.title: +MT on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +MT on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Food poisons
item.description:
Two Marine polyether polyketide toxins produced by dinoflagellates that are then taken up by bivalves. When people eat the bivalve they can be poisoned by them.

Note that cooking, freezing, or other preparation methods do not destroy heat-stable polyether toxins like okadaic acid, so avoidance of suspect sources is essential.
